items.title: Google DeepMind | Research profile | Nature Index
items.author: www.nature.com
items.snippet: Google DeepMind has been highly active in early 2026 with a range of research publications spanning mathematics, AI safety, fluid dynamics, and scientific discovery. A standout initiative is Gemini Deep Think, an advanced reasoning model that has significantly outperformed previous IMO-Gold benchmarks and is being positioned as a scientific companion for researchers across mathematics, physics, and computer science. DeepMind published two cross-disciplinary papers in late March/early April 2026

items.title: Gemma 4 — Google DeepMind
items.author: deepmind.google
items.snippet: Google AI and Google DeepMind have been highly active in April 2026 with several major announcements. The headline release is Gemma 4, launched on April 2, 2026 — an open-weight model family available under the Apache 2.0 license, shipping in four sizes (E2B, E4B, 26B MoE, and 31B Dense). Gemma 4 is built for agentic and autonomous AI workflows running on-device, featuring multimodal capabilities (text + image, with audio on edge variants), 256K token context windows, support for 140+ languages,

items.title: Deploy to Any Platform | Genkit - Firebase - Google
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Firebase Genkit is an open-source AI framework by Google available in JavaScript, Go, and Python that provides unified APIs for working with AI models such as Gemini, GPT, and Claude. The framework includes built-in developer tools, a local Developer UI for testing flows, structured output support, tool calling, streaming, and production monitoring via Genkit Monitoring. The documentation covers everything from getting started guides to advanced topics like RAG (retrieval-augmented generation),

items.title: GitHub - google-gemini/gemini-cli: An open-source AI agent that brings the power of Gemini directly into your terminal
items.author: github.com
items.snippet:
Google's Gemini CLI (github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli) has been actively releasing updates into 2026, with release notes updated as recently as January 13-14, 2026. The repository is an open-source AI agent that brings Gemini directly into the terminal, and it has seen frequent changelog updates reflecting rapid development. A notable addition is the experimental browser agent capability, web fetch improvements, and SDK/custom skills support.

On the model side, Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview was rel

items.title: Gemini for Google Cloud is here | Google Cloud Blog
items.author: cloud.google.com
items.snippet:
Google Cloud has made several major Gemini AI announcements in early 2026. Gemini 3.1 Pro launched in preview on Vertex AI and Gemini Enterprise, positioned as a noticeably smarter baseline for complex reasoning and problem-solving, accessible via the Gemini API, Google AI Studio, and Gemini CLI. Alongside it, Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite debuted as the fastest and most cost-efficient model in the Gemini 3 series, targeting high-volume developer workloads at scale.

Google also launched Gemini Live API

items.title: Leveling up your developer experience in Google AI Studio
items.author: blog.google
items.snippet: Google AI Studio has undergone a major transformation in early 2026, most notably through the integration of the Antigravity coding agent and Firebase support into a unified full-stack development pipeline. The Antigravity agent introduces an "agent-first" paradigm where autonomous AI agents can build entire applications from natural language prompts — handling database creation, Firestore setup, security rules, and Firebase Authentication configuration automatically. items.title: Genkit - Open-source AI framework by Google (Firebase Docs)
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Genkit is Google's open-source framework for building AI-powered applications in JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Python. It provides a code-centric approach to AI development with built-in primitives for chat, RAG (retrieval-augmented generation), tool use, and agentic workflows. The framework includes a CLI and local Developer UI for rapid testing and iteration, unified APIs for text generation, structured output, streaming, and tool calling across supported languages, plus observability and eva

items.title: Genkit Overview
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Genkit is Google's open-source framework for building AI-powered applications in JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Python. It provides a code-centric approach to AI development with built-in primitives for chat, RAG (retrieval-augmented generation), tool use, and agentic workflows. The framework includes a CLI and local Developer UI for rapid testing and iteration, unified APIs for text generation, structured output, streaming, and tool calling across supported languages, plus observability and eva
